title: RDF / OWL — TBox then ABox
description: Build a warranty ontology: classes, properties, individuals, Turtle serialization.
story: Imagine a warehouse with one rule book on the wall (TBox): shelves may only be labeled Product, Symptom, FailureMode, Part. Trucks deliver boxes of facts about specific machines (ABox). You never print a new rule book for each SKU — you shelve new boxes under existing labels. RDF is the triple language for both; OWL adds logical rules.
one_liner: TBox = rule book (classes/properties); ABox = shelves (instances); RDF triples for both.

beats.title: TBox Part A — Classes with rdflib
beats.narrative: Three triples per class: type, label, comment. This is the pattern — memorize it.
beats.code:
from rdflib import Graph, Namespace, RDF, RDFS, OWL, XSD, Literal, URIRef

WD = Namespace("https://example.org/warranty-diagnosis#")
g = Graph()
g.bind("wd", WD)
g.bind("owl", OWL)
g.bind("rdfs", RDFS)

# Every class is three triples: type, label, comment
g.add((WD.Product, RDF.type, OWL.Class))
g.add((WD.Product, RDFS.label, Literal("Product")))
g.add((WD.Product, RDFS.comment, Literal("Appliance product family")))

g.add((WD.Symptom, RDF.type, OWL.Class))
g.add((WD.FailureMode, RDF.type, OWL.Class))
g.add((WD.Part, RDF.type, OWL.Class))

annotations.note: rdflib Graph is an in-memory triple store.
Namespace keeps IRIs short and consistent (wd:Product).
Empty Graph(); bind prefixes for readable Turtle later.
OWL.Class declares a TBox class — not an instance.

choices: WD.Product is an instance of a washing machine

beats.title: TBox Part B — Object & Datatype properties
beats.narrative: ObjectProperty = edge between resources. DatatypeProperty = literal attributes.
beats.code:
# ObjectProperty = edge between two resources (URI → URI)
g.add((WD.indicates, RDF.type, OWL.ObjectProperty))
g.add((WD.indicates, RDFS.domain, WD.Symptom))
g.add((WD.indicates, RDFS.range, WD.FailureMode))
g.add((WD.indicates, RDFS.label, Literal("indicates")))

# DatatypeProperty = attribute with a literal
g.add((WD.confidence, RDF.type, OWL.DatatypeProperty))
g.add((WD.confidence, RDFS.domain, WD.indicates))  # often on reified axiom
g.add((WD.confidence, RDFS.range, XSD.decimal))

annotations.note: DatatypeProperty points to XSD literals (numbers, strings).

line_quiz.prompt: indicates is which kind of property?

fill_blanks.template:
g.add((WD.indicates, RDF.type, {{p1}}))
g.add((WD.indicates, RDFS.domain, {{p2}}))
g.add((WD.indicates, RDFS.range, {{p3}}))

beats.title: ABox — individuals and INDICATES
beats.narrative: Same Graph API; RDF.type points at your TBox class, not owl:Class.
beats.code:
# ABox: individuals under TBox classes
wm = WD["product_wm_001"]
g.add((wm, RDF.type, WD.Product))
g.add((wm, RDFS.label, Literal("Front Load Washer 8kg")))

s = WD["sym_wm_s01"]
g.add((s, RDF.type, WD.Symptom))
g.add((s, RDFS.label, Literal("Will not drain")))

fm = WD["fm_wm_fm01"]
g.add((fm, RDF.type, WD.FailureMode))
g.add((s, WD.indicates, fm))  # plain triple
# confidence often stored via owl:Axiom reification in richer models

annotations.note: Individual IRI under the product namespace.
Type is WD.Product — ABox fact under TBox class.
Predicate WD.indicates is the TBox ObjectProperty.

beats.title: Turtle serialization — read what you built
beats.narrative: If you can hand-write this Turtle from memory, you understand TBox+ABox.

annotations.note: Prefix maps short names to IRIs.
a owl:Class is Turtle sugar for rdf:type.

self_quiz.q: Why not create a new OWL file per product SKU?
self_quiz.a: New product is ABox under shared TBox; per-SKU schema does not scale.
Object links resources; Datatype links to literals (XSD).

self_quiz.q: Where does runtime diagnosis usually run?
self_quiz.a: Neo4j property graph (operational ABox); RDF/OWL for formal interchange.

common_mistakes: Typing an instance as owl:Class instead of your domain class.
Inventing property names not in the TBox.
Thinking Turtle file is what the chat queries at runtime.
final_boss: From blank page: create Product, Symptom, FailureMode, indicates in rdflib.
Explain TBox vs ABox with the warehouse story in 45 seconds.
